## Acceptance Criteria

Pass. `.jit/gates.json:193` now runs `jit validate "$JIT_ISSUE_ID"`, which invokes the existing per-issue path. `crates/jit/src/gate_execution.rs:53` sets `JIT_ISSUE_ID`, `crates/jit/src/main.rs:4603-4608` dispatches `jit validate <id>` to `executor.run_rules(id.as_deref())`, and `crates/jit/src/commands/validate.rs:944-982` filters findings to the target issue.

No stale `jit-validate` preset defining bare `jit validate` was found. The remaining `--scope` references are for the separate coverage-preview flow.

## Architecture And Separation

Pass. The implementation is a gate registry/configuration change in `.jit/gates.json`; it reuses existing CLI and command paths. No domain/storage/commands/CLI boundary violation was introduced.

## Functional Paradigm

Pass. No Rust implementation logic was added for this issue.

## Correctness And Invariants

Pass. The per-issue validation gate no longer runs whole-repo validation and does not use container-subtree `--scope`. No atomic-write, event-log, gate-transition, assignee/label-format, or PID-safety regression was introduced by the scoped config change.

Pass. `#![deny(unsafe_code)]` remains present in the Rust crate entry points, and no unsafe code was introduced.

I did run the required `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`; it passed with `Repository validation passed`.
